There are 0 high-risk steps (3 medium at most), and while steps 3-7 are logically parallel, they cannot be independently written to the same file. Both prior phases (questions, research) independently concluded PROCEED_ALONE for the same reason."

1	[Task api-docs-001] PHASE 3b: IMPLEMENT
2	Title: Add OpenAPI specification and API documentation
3	
4	Read your plan at .fleet/tasks/api-docs-001/plan.json.
5	
6	YOU MUST IMPLEMENT the plan completely. This is the critical execution phase.
7	
8	For EACH step in plan.json, you MUST:
9	1. Announce: "=== Step N: {task description} ==="
10	2. Execute the step — create/modify the actual source files
11	3. Verify it worked (check file exists, run code, test passes)
12	4. Announce: "=== Step N complete ===" before moving to next
13	
14	DO NOT STOP until ALL steps are executed and verified.
15	DO NOT just read the plan — you must CREATE the files it specifies.
16	
17	DEPENDENCIES: Fresh worktree — install before testing:
18	  - Node: pnpm install or npm install
19	  - Python: pip install -r requirements.txt
20	
21	After all steps are complete, write .fleet/tasks/api-docs-001/implementation.json:
22	{
23	  "phase": "implement",
24	  "steps_completed": [
25	    { "step": 1, "task": "...", "files_created": ["..."], "files_modified": ["..."], "verified": true }
26	  ],
27	  "all_steps_done": true
28	}
29	
30	BEFORE YOU STOP: git add -A && git commit --no-verify -m "execute: api-docs-001"

file.content:
[Task api-docs-001] PHASE 3b: IMPLEMENT
Title: Add OpenAPI specification and API documentation

Read your plan at .fleet/tasks/api-docs-001/plan.json.

YOU MUST IMPLEMENT the plan completely. This is the critical execution phase.

For EACH step in plan.json, you MUST:
1. Announce: "=== Step N: {task description} ==="
2. Execute the step — create/modify the actual source files
3. Verify it worked (check file exists, run code, test passes)
4. Announce: "=== Step N complete ===" before moving to next

DO NOT STOP until ALL steps are executed and verified.
DO NOT just read the plan — you must CREATE the files it specifies.

DEPENDENCIES: Fresh worktree — install before testing:
  - Node: pnpm install or npm install
  - Python: pip install -r requirements.txt

After all steps are complete, write .fleet/tasks/api-docs-001/implementation.json:
{
  "phase": "implement",
  "steps_completed": [
    { "step": 1, "task": "...", "files_created": ["..."], "files_modified": ["..."], "verified": true }
  ],
  "all_steps_done": true
}

BEFORE YOU STOP: git add -A && git commit --no-verify -m "execute: api-docs-001"

1	# T544 — Bayes Event-Sourcing Contract (commands / events / snapshots)
2	
3	Status: draft v1 (canonical)  
4	Scope: deterministic replay contract for Bayes MVP
5	
6	## 1) Purpose
7	
8	Define strict wire/storage contracts for:
9	
10	- command envelope (ingress intent)
11	- event envelope (accepted/rejected outcomes)
12	- snapshot envelope (state checkpoints)
13	
14	These contracts are designed so replay from `snapshot + ordered events` is deterministic and auditable.
15	
16	---
17	
18	## 2) Contract IDs
19	
20	- Command envelope: `bayes-command/v1`
21	- Event envelope: `bayes-event/v1`
22	- Snapshot envelope: `bayes-snapshot/v1`
23	- Replay hash profile: `bayes-replay-hash/v1`
24	
25	All persisted records MUST carry `schemaVersion` equal to one of these IDs.
26	
27	---
28	
29	## 3) Command Envelope (`bayes-command/v1`)
30	
31	```json
32	{
33	  "schemaVersion": "bayes-command/v1",
34	  "commandId": "cmd_01J...",
35	  "marketId": "market_demo_001",
36	  "accountId": "acct_abc",
37	  "commandType": "ProbabilityEdit",
38	  "idempotencyKey": "optional-client-key",
39	  "submittedAt": "2026-02-26T00:00:00Z",
40	  "payload": {},
41	  "meta": {
42	    "source": "api",
43	    "ipHash": "sha256:...",
44	    "userAgent": "..."
45	  }
46	}
47	```
48	
49	### Required fields
50	
51	- `schemaVersion`
52	- `commandId` (globally unique)
53	- `marketId`
54	- `accountId`
55	- `commandType` (`ProbabilityEdit | EventTrade | AdminOp`)
56	- `submittedAt` (ISO-8601 UTC)
57	- `payload` (command-specific object)
58	
59	### Command payload variants
60	
61	## 3.1 `ProbabilityEdit`
62	
63	```json
64	{
65	  "variableId": "ai_policy_passes_2027",
66	  "target": { "kind": "marginal", "outcomeId": "yes", "probability": 0.63 },
67	  "context": []
68	}
69	```
70	
71	- `context` holds conditional assignments (empty for unconditional edits).
72	
73	## 3.2 `EventTrade`
74	
75	```json
76	{
77	  "formula": [
78	    [
79	      { "variableId": "m1", "outcomeId": "yes", "negated": false }
80	    ]
81	  ],
82	  "size": 12.5,
83	  "side": "buy"
84	}
85	```
86	
87	- Canonical payload shape is nested-array CNF: `list[list[{variableId,outcomeId,negated}]]`.
88	- This document freezes the materialized command payload as stored/returned by the current server, so `formula[*][*].variableId` is the public market id (for example `m1`), not the internal `market["variableId"]`.
89	- During shared validation, the server temporarily translates those market ids to internal variable ids, validates outcomes/structure, then restores market ids before persisting the command payload.
90	- Structurally invalid formulas fail validation with `400 invalid_event_formula`.
91	- Structurally valid but currently unsupported non-atomic CNF remains schema-valid and is rejected later with `501 event_trade_inference_unavailable`.
92	- Current executable subset is exactly one clause containing one non-negated literal.
93	- The older `{"kind":"CNF","expr":"..."}` representation is obsolete for the live checkout.
94	
95	## 3.3 `AdminOp`
96	
97	Reserved for deterministic operator actions (e.g., market freeze) and must be explicitly whitelisted.
98	
99	---
100	
101	## 4) Event Envelope (`bayes-event/v1`)
102	
103	Every sequenced command emits exactly one terminal event:
104	
105	- `CommandAccepted`
106	- `CommandRejected`
107	
108	Optional intermediate engine events may be emitted but must not replace the terminal event.
109	
110	```json
111	{
112	  "schemaVersion": "bayes-event/v1",
113	  "eventId": "evt_01J...",
114	  "marketId": "market_demo_001",
115	  "seq": 1042,
116	  "commandId": "cmd_01J...",
117	  "eventType": "CommandAccepted",
118	  "emittedAt": "2026-02-26T00:00:01Z",
119	  "approxFlag": false,
120	  "payload": {},
121	  "prevEventHash": "sha256:...",
122	  "eventHash": "sha256:..."
123	}
124	```
125	
126	### Required fields
127	
128	- `schemaVersion`
129	- `eventId` (globally unique)
130	- `marketId`
131	- `seq` (strictly increasing integer per market shard)
132	- `commandId`
133	- `eventType`
134	- `emittedAt` (ISO-8601 UTC)
135	- `payload`
136	- `prevEventHash`
137	- `eventHash`
138	
139	### `CommandAccepted` payload
140	
141	```json
142	{
143	  "effects": {
144	    "marginalDelta": [{ "variableId": "ai_policy_passes_2027", "before": 0.58, "after": 0.63 }],
145	    "assetDelta": [{ "accountId": "acct_abc", "beforeMinAsset": 3.20, "afterMinAsset": 2.91 }]
146	  },
147	  "pricing": { "cost": 1.74, "fee": 0.02 },
148	  "replayStateHash": "sha256:..."
149	}
150	```
151	
152	### `CommandRejected` payload
153	
154	```json
155	{
156	  "reasonCode": "min_asset_violation",
157	  "reason": "Edit would produce negative state-contingent assets",
158	  "retryHint": "reduce probability target"
159	}
160	```
161	
162	---
163	
164	## 5) Snapshot Envelope (`bayes-snapshot/v1`)
165	
166	```json
167	{
168	  "schemaVersion": "bayes-snapshot/v1",
169	  "snapshotId": "snp_01J...",
170	  "marketId": "market_demo_001",
171	  "seq": 1000,
172	  "takenAt": "2026-02-26T00:10:00Z",
173	  "state": {
174	    "bnFactors": {},
175	    "marginals": {},
176	    "assets": {},
177	    "engineMode": "EXACT"
178	  },
179	  "stateHash": "sha256:...",
180	  "lastEventHash": "sha256:...",
181	  "replayHashProfile": "bayes-replay-hash/v1"
182	}
183	```
184	
185	### Required fields
186	
187	- `schemaVersion`
188	- `snapshotId`
189	- `marketId`
190	- `seq` (last included event sequence)
191	- `takenAt` (ISO-8601 UTC)
192	- `state`
193	- `stateHash`
194	- `lastEventHash`
195	- `replayHashProfile`
196	
197	---
198	
199	## 6) Determinism Rules (MUST)
200	
201	1. **Single writer per market shard**: only sequencer assigns `seq`.
202	2. **Total ordering**: replay applies events strictly by `seq` ascending.
203	3. **Idempotent ingest**: duplicate `commandId` returns prior terminal event outcome.
204	4. **Terminal event guarantee**: each accepted command path emits exactly one terminal event record.
205	5. **Hash-chain integrity**:
206	   - `prevEventHash` equals previous event’s `eventHash` (same market).
207	   - `eventHash` computed from canonical JSON serialization under `bayes-replay-hash/v1`.
208	6. **Snapshot continuity**:
209	   - snapshot `lastEventHash` must match event at snapshot `seq`.
210	7. **No wall-clock dependence in transition logic**:
211	   - timestamps are metadata only; state transitions use command payload + prior state + deterministic config.
212	
213	---
214	
215	## 7) Versioning Policy
216	
217	## 7.1 Backward-compatible changes (same v1)
218	
219	Allowed:
220	- add optional fields
221	- add optional event payload subfields
222	- add non-breaking reason codes
223	
224	Forbidden in v1:
225	- rename/remove required fields
226	- change required field types
227	- change hash canonicalization algorithm
228	
229	## 7.2 Breaking changes
230	
231	Any breaking change requires new contract ID:
232	- `bayes-command/v2`
233	- `bayes-event/v2`
234	- `bayes-snapshot/v2`
235	
236	Migration rule:
237	- mixed-version replay in one stream is disallowed unless explicit converter is version-pinned and audited.
238	
239	---
240	
241	## 8) Canonical Serialization / Hash Profile (`bayes-replay-hash/v1`)
242	
243	Hash inputs use canonical JSON:
244	
245	- UTF-8 encoding
246	- lexicographically sorted object keys
247	- no insignificant whitespace
248	- numbers serialized in normalized decimal form
249	- hash algorithm: `sha256`
250	- encoded as `sha256:<hex>`
251	
252	Objects hashed:
253	- event envelope minus `eventHash` field (for computing `eventHash`)
254	- snapshot `state` object (for `stateHash`)
255	
256	---
257	
258	## 9) Storage Index Requirements
259	
260	Minimum DB indexes for replay and audit:
261	
262	- events: `(marketId, seq)` unique
263	- events: `commandId` unique per market
264	- snapshots: `(marketId, seq)` unique
265	- snapshots: `snapshotId` unique
266	
267	---
268	
269	## 10) Acceptance Checkpoints for T544
270	
271	- [ ] Command/event/snapshot envelopes documented with required fields
272	- [ ] Determinism rules explicitly listed (single-writer, total order, idempotency, hash-chain)
273	- [ ] Versioning policy defined (non-breaking vs breaking)
274	- [ ] Hash profile documented for replay reproducibility
275	- [ ] Storage index requirements specified for implementation handoff
276	
277	---
278	
279	## 11) Implementation handoff notes
280	
281	This document is the contract reference for:
282	- T545 (deterministic replay hash strategy)
283	- T562/T563/T564/T565 (sequencer, journal, snapshot, replay)
284	- T568+ API contract tasks consuming command/event metadata
285	- T547/T570 EventTrade payload and route behavior; see `t547-shared-event-formula-contract-freeze.md` for the validator-specific freeze behind this persisted command shape
286	
287	Any deviation from these contracts must be recorded with:
288	1) explicit rationale, 2) impacted tasks, 3) migration strategy, 4) new contract version if breaking.
289

1	# T586 Full-Chain HTTP Contract Freeze
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-05
4	Branch: `ff/Ttemporal-fleet-cy8-r1-bayes-t586-freeze-full-chain-http-contract`
5	
6	## Question
7	
8	Before extending the full-chain integration coverage in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`, what HTTP contract is already frozen for:
9	
10	- accepted `POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/probability-edit`
11	- unconditional solvency rejections on the same route
12	
13	This checkpoint exists to stop a test-only task from silently rewriting the live server contract.
14	
15	## Findings
16	
17	### 1. Accepted ProbabilityEdit submissions already return `201`, not `200`
18	
19	The route implementation hard-codes `201` at the acceptance boundary.
20	
21	Evidence:
22	
23	- `build_terminal_acceptance_response(...)` in `apps/bayes-market/backend/server.py` records terminal outcomes with status `201` and returns `(response, 201)`.
24	- The HTTP integration suite asserts `201` for the market-scoped route, idempotent replay, zero-headroom acceptance, and read-after-write risk flow in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`.
25	- The earlier HTTP freeze memo `apps/bayes-market/docs/t1737-probability-edit-http-contract-freeze.md` already froze the response shape as top-level `201` with `{order, meta}`.
26	
27	This is not an incidental test expectation. It is the explicit server response contract.
28	
29	### 2. Unconditional solvency failures are terminal domain rejections with `409`, not request-validation `400`
30	
31	The unconditional solvency branch runs only after the request body has already passed structural validation and has been normalized into a canonical ProbabilityEdit command. The server then previews the edit and rejects it only if `afterMinAsset < 0`.
32	
33	Evidence:
34	
35	- `handle_probability_edit(...)` in `apps/bayes-market/backend/server.py` raises `400 invalid_probability_edit` only for malformed request fields such as missing `accountId` or bad `target.probability`.
36	- The same handler calls `build_terminal_rejection_response(...)` with `code="min_asset_violation"` and `status=409` when the previewed unconditional edit would drive `afterMinAsset` negative.
37	- `build_terminal_rejection_response(...)` emits a `CommandRejected` event, mirrors the symbolic code into both `error.code` and `result.reasonCode`, and records a replayable terminal outcome.
38	
39	That behavior makes the current `409` meaningful: the command is well-formed, but the server refuses to accept it as a valid state transition.
40	
41	### 3. `min_asset_violation` is the canonical rejection code already shared by code, tests, and event-contract docs
42	
43	There is no in-tree evidence for `insufficient_min_asset`.
44	
45	Evidence:
46	
47	- `apps/bayes-market/backend/server.py` uses `code="min_asset_violation"` for unconditional solvency failures.
48	- `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` asserts `error.code == "min_asset_violation"` and, on direct route coverage, `result.reasonCode == "min_asset_violation"`.
49	- `apps/bayes-market/docs/t544-event-sourcing-contract.md` freezes the `CommandRejected` payload example with `"reasonCode": "min_asset_violation"`.
50	- Repo-wide search found no Bayes contract source using `insufficient_min_asset`.
51	
52	Renaming the rejection code would therefore be a real contract change across the HTTP layer, event payloads, and existing tests.
53	
54	### 4. The current full-chain test surface already depends on the existing contract
55	
56	The integration file already covers the edit -> risk-state update path and the unconditional solvency rejection path under the current HTTP contract.
57	
58	Evidence:
59	
60	- `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` asserts `201` for successful HTTP ProbabilityEdit submissions and then reads `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/risk` to verify the asset update.
61	- The same file asserts `409` plus `min_asset_violation` for unconditional solvency failure and verifies that market state, account state, orders, and replay metadata remain unchanged except for the recorded terminal rejection.
62	
63	Appending new assertions under T586 should extend this surface, not contradict it.
64	
65	### 5. Reproducible branch runtime matches the frozen contract
66	
67	Verification run:
68	
69	```bash
70	python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_bayes_market_api.py'
71	```
72	
73	Result: `Ran 62 tests ... OK`
74	
75	That suite includes the HTTP integration and solvency property coverage described above, so the checked-in runtime agrees with the source-level contract.
76	
77	### 6. There is no local deployed Bayes listener on `127.0.0.1:3200` in this worktree session
78	
79	On this host, `127.0.0.1:3200` is currently a Node listener and `GET /healthz` returns `404 {"error":"Not found"}`. The existing public probe script also checks only `/healthz`, not the ProbabilityEdit POST contract.
80	
81	Implication:
82	
83	- this checkpoint is frozen from checked-in server code plus reproducible test runtime
84	- it is not a claim that this specific local host is currently serving Bayes on `:3200`
85	
86	## Freeze Decision For T586
87	
88	Until there is an explicit server-contract rewrite task, the safe assertions for `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` are:
89	
90	- accepted ProbabilityEdit HTTP writes return `201`
91	- unconditional solvency failures return `409`
92	- unconditional solvency failures use `error.code == "min_asset_violation"`
93	- terminal rejection metadata also keeps `result.reasonCode == "min_asset_violation"`
94	- `400` remains reserved for malformed request/query validation, not for a structurally valid command that fails solvency checks
95	
96	## Consequence
97	
98	The mismatch is between the current task brief wording and the actual Bayes HTTP contract already encoded in source, tests, and contract docs.
99	
100	For this branch, treat `200` and `400/insufficient_min_asset` as out-of-contract expectations. If those semantics are desired, they should be introduced only through a separate server-contract change that updates:
101	
102	- `apps/bayes-market/backend/server.py`
103	- existing HTTP/property tests in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`
104	- event-contract docs in `apps/bayes-market/docs/t544-event-sourcing-contract.md`
105	- HTTP freeze docs in `apps/bayes-market/docs/t1737-probability-edit-http-contract-freeze.md`
106	
107	That keeps T586 as a test-coverage task instead of turning it into an unplanned API rewrite.
108

1	# T1737 ProbabilityEdit HTTP Contract Freeze
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-05
4	Branch: `ff/Ttemporal-fleet-d6g-r4-bayes-t1737-freeze-http-command-boundary`
5	
6	## Question
7	
8	For `POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/probability-edit`, should the public HTTP route accept the full canonical `bayes-command/v1` envelope, or should it accept a thinner REST body and let the server construct the canonical command envelope internally?
9	
10	## Decision
11	
12	Freeze the public HTTP contract as a **thin REST body**, not the full `bayes-command/v1` envelope.
13	
14	`bayes-command/v1` remains the canonical ingest and persistence contract, but it should be materialized **behind** the HTTP boundary by the route handler before the command reaches the sequencer/journal layer.
15	
16	At the public boundary, the request body is the ProbabilityEdit payload plus route-local request metadata:
17	
18	- required `accountId`
19	- optional `idempotencyKey`
20	- `variableId`
21	- `target`
22	- `context`
23	
24	## Why This Is The Right Boundary
25	
26	### 1. The restored route already behaves like a thin REST endpoint
27	
28	The current restored handler validates the ProbabilityEdit payload fields, requires a top-level `accountId`, accepts an optional top-level `idempotencyKey`, and still does not read `schemaVersion`, `commandId`, `marketId`, `commandType`, or `submittedAt` from the request body.
29	
30	Evidence:
31	
32	- [`apps/bayes-market/backend/server.py`](../backend/server.py) validates `variableId`, `target`, and `context` from the request payload and derives command metadata internally.
33	- [`apps/bayes-market/backend/server.py`](../backend/server.py) requires `accountId`, accepts optional `idempotencyKey`, and generates `commandId` and `submittedAt` server-side.
34	- [`tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`](../../../tests/test_bayes_market_api.py) sends only `accountId`, optional `idempotencyKey`, `variableId`, `target`, and `context`.
35	
36	### 2. The canonical Bayes command envelope is broader than the HTTP payload
37	
38	The event-sourcing contract defines `bayes-command/v1` as the canonical command envelope with metadata required for deterministic ingest and replay: `schemaVersion`, `commandId`, `marketId`, `accountId`, `commandType`, `submittedAt`, and `payload`.
39	
40	Evidence:
41	
42	- [`apps/bayes-market/docs/t544-event-sourcing-contract.md`](t544-event-sourcing-contract.md) defines the envelope.
43	- [`apps/bayes-market/docs/t544-event-sourcing-contract.md`](t544-event-sourcing-contract.md) lists the required fields.
44	- [`apps/bayes-market/docs/t544-event-sourcing-contract.md`](t544-event-sourcing-contract.md) defines the ProbabilityEdit payload as the `payload` sub-object, not as the whole envelope.
45	
46	### 3. The planning docs separate payload schemas from persistence contracts
47	
48	The Bayes planning docs describe command work in two layers:
49	
50	- payload schemas for ProbabilityEdit/EventTrade
51	- event-sourcing persistence contracts for commands/events/snapshots
52	
53	That split is consistent with a thin HTTP payload that is wrapped into `bayes-command/v1` internally.
54	
55	Evidence:
56	
57	- [`apps/bayes-market/docs/t538-scope-matrix.md`](t538-scope-matrix.md) calls out "ProbabilityEdit and EventTrade payload schemas".
58	- [`apps/bayes-market/docs/t538-scope-matrix.md`](t538-scope-matrix.md) separately calls out the event-sourced journal/snapshot/replay contract.
59	- [`apps/bayes-market/docs/t537-epic-execution-baseline.md`](t537-epic-execution-baseline.md) splits T544 from T546.
60	
61	### 4. The branch history shows the full-envelope HTTP route was superseded
62	
63	`task/T569` required the full `bayes-command/v1` envelope on the HTTP route, including body `marketId` matching the path. The later `task/T1736` alignment removed that requirement and changed the route to accept the thinner payload body that is restored on this branch.
64	
65	That makes `task/T1736`, not `task/T569`, the last explicit decision point for the public HTTP boundary.
66	
67	More precisely:
68	
69	- `task/T569` treated the HTTP request as the canonical command envelope and rejected requests missing `schemaVersion`, `commandId`, `marketId`, `accountId`, `commandType`, `submittedAt`, or `payload`.
70	- `task/T1736` switched the public route to a market-scoped body, reading `marketId` from the path and `payload` fields directly from the JSON body.
71	- the restored stub on this branch keeps the T1736 thin-body boundary and tightens `accountId` from a T1736 defaulted field to an explicitly required client-supplied field.
72	
73	### 5. The response contract is REST submission state, not the canonical envelope
74	
75	The restored route returns `201` with `{order, meta}` where `order` is an execution-facing submission record. It does not return the raw `bayes-command/v1` envelope.
76	
77	Evidence:
78	
79	- [`apps/bayes-market/backend/server.py`](../backend/server.py) builds an `order` object containing execution details such as `previousMarginals`, `newMarginals`, `impactScore`, `createdAt`, and `filledAt`.
80	- [`apps/bayes-market/backend/server.py`](../backend/server.py) wraps that order in `{"order": ..., "meta": ...}`.
81	- [`tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`](../../../tests/test_bayes_market_api.py) asserts `payload["order"]`, `payload["meta"]`, `payload["order"]["commandId"]`, `payload["order"]["submittedAt"]`, and `payload["meta"]["idempotencyKeyEcho"]`.
82	
83	## Frozen Field Ownership
84	
85	| Field | Public HTTP request | Internal `bayes-command/v1` | Freeze |
86	|---|---|---|---|
87	| `marketId` | Not supplied in JSON body | Server sets from `/v1/markets/{id}` path segment | **Server-generated** |
88	| `accountId` | Required top-level JSON field on this route | Copied into envelope `accountId` | **Client-supplied** for this MVP route |
89	| `commandId` | Not accepted from client | Server generates at accept time | **Server-generated** |
90	| `submittedAt` | Not accepted from client | Server stamps at accept time | **Server-generated** |
91	| `idempotencyKey` | Optional top-level JSON field | Copied into envelope if present | **Client-supplied when present** |
92	| `commandType` | Not supplied in JSON body | Server sets to `ProbabilityEdit` from the route | **Server-generated** |
93	| `schemaVersion` | Not supplied in JSON body | Server sets to `bayes-command/v1` | **Server-generated** |
94	
95	## Expected Request Shape
96	
97	The frozen HTTP request body should be:
98	
99	```json
100	{
101	  "accountId": "acct_abc",
102	  "idempotencyKey": "optional-client-key",
103	  "variableId": "eth_price_gt_3000_mar15",
104	  "target": { "kind": "marginal", "outcomeId": "yes", "probability": 0.8 },
105	  "context": []
106	}
107	```
108	
109	The server should translate that into an internal command envelope shaped like:
110	
111	```json
112	{
113	  "schemaVersion": "bayes-command/v1",
114	  "commandId": "cmd_01J...",
115	  "marketId": "m1",
116	  "accountId": "acct_abc",
117	  "commandType": "ProbabilityEdit",
118	  "idempotencyKey": "optional-client-key",
119	  "submittedAt": "2026-04-05T00:00:00Z",
120	  "payload": {
121	    "variableId": "eth_price_gt_3000_mar15",
122	    "target": { "kind": "marginal", "outcomeId": "yes", "probability": 0.8 },
123	    "context": []
124	  }
125	}
126	```
127	
128	## Expected Response Shape
129	
130	Keep the current REST-style submission response shape: top-level `201` with `{order, meta}`, not a raw `bayes-command/v1` document.
131	
132	The `order` object is the route response model and may include execution-facing fields beyond the canonical command envelope. On the restored stub, those fields are already part of the response contract:
133	
134	```json
135	{
136	  "order": {
137	    "id": "ord_...",
138	    "type": "ProbabilityEdit",
139	    "marketId": "m1",
140	    "accountId": "acct_abc",
141	    "status": "filled",
142	    "payload": {
143	      "variableId": "eth_price_gt_3000_mar15",
144	      "target": { "kind": "marginal", "outcomeId": "yes", "probability": 0.8 },
145	      "context": []
146	    },
147	    "previousMarginals": { "yes": 0.65, "no": 0.35 },
148	    "newMarginals": { "yes": 0.8, "no": 0.2 },
149	    "impactScore": 0.068148,
150	    "commandId": "cmd_01J...",
151	    "submittedAt": "2026-04-05T00:00:00Z",
152	    "createdAt": "2026-04-05T00:00:00Z",
153	    "filledAt": "2026-04-05T00:00:00Z",
154	    "idempotencyKey": "optional-client-key"
155	  },
156	  "meta": {
157	    "timestamp": "2026-04-05T00:00:00Z",
158	    "idempotencyKeyEcho": "optional-client-key"
159	  }
160	}
161	```
162	
163	Freeze points:
164	
165	- Keep `201` with `{order, meta}` rather than returning the raw `bayes-command/v1` envelope.
166	- Do not make clients send command metadata just to get it echoed back.
167	- Surface server-generated `commandId` and `submittedAt` in the response so clients can correlate retries and downstream audit records.
168	- Keep `order.marketId` and `order.accountId` as echoed submission attributes in the response resource rather than as top-level canonical-envelope fields.
169	- If an idempotency key is supplied, echo it back in `meta.idempotencyKeyEcho`, matching the wider API convention in [`docs/ops/futarchy-web-redo/meta6/unified-endpoint-contract-spec.md`](../../../docs/ops/futarchy-web-redo/meta6/unified-endpoint-contract-spec.md).
170	
171	## Why Not Revert To The Full Envelope At The HTTP Boundary
172	
173	Reviving the `task/T569` full-envelope request shape would be the wrong freeze for this branch because:
174	
175	1. It conflicts with the restored source and tests that currently define the route.
176	2. It duplicates path-scoped `marketId` in the body and reintroduces an avoidable mismatch class.
177	3. It exposes internal sequencing metadata (`schemaVersion`, `commandId`, `submittedAt`, `commandType`) as public request requirements even though the route already fixes or can derive them.
178	4. It collapses the distinction between the public REST surface and the canonical ingest contract that T544/T538 already separate.
179	
180	## Current Stub Gap
181	
182	The restored stub is only a **partial** implementation of the frozen contract:
183	
184	- It already uses the thin request body shape.
185	- It now requires `accountId`, echoes optional `idempotencyKey`, and returns server-generated `commandId` and `submittedAt`.
186	- It still rejects any non-empty `context` instead of validating and normalizing conditional edits.
187	- It still does **not** materialize or persist a full internal `bayes-command/v1` envelope behind the HTTP boundary.
188	- It still does **not** implement idempotent retry handling or explicit terminal rejection/result semantics.
189	
190	That gap is acceptable for this analysis task, but it should be treated as a follow-up implementation delta rather than as evidence that the public route should revert to the full canonical envelope.

74	def ws_accept_key(client_key: str) -> str:
75	    """Compute Sec-WebSocket-Accept from Sec-WebSocket-Key per RFC 6455."""
76	    digest = hashlib.sha1(client_key.encode() + _WS_MAGIC).digest()  # noqa: S324
77	    return base64.b64encode(digest).decode()

80	def ws_read_frame(sock: Any) -> tuple[int, bytes] | None:
81	    """Read one WebSocket frame, unmask the payload. Returns (opcode, data) or None on EOF."""
82	    header = _recv_exact(sock, 2)
83	    if header is None:
84	        return None
85	    opcode = header[0] & 0x0F
86	    masked = bool(header[1] & 0x80)
87	    length = header[1] & 0x7F
88	    if length == 126:
89	        raw = _recv_exact(sock, 2)
90	        if raw is None:
91	            return None
92	        length = struct.unpack("!H", raw)[0]
93	    elif length == 127:
94	        raw = _recv_exact(sock, 8)
95	        if raw is None:
96	            return None
97	        length = struct.unpack("!Q", raw)[0]
98	    mask_key = b""
99	    if masked:
100	        mask_key = _recv_exact(sock, 4)
101	        if mask_key is None:
102	            return None
103	    payload = _recv_exact(sock, length)
104	    if payload is None:
105	        return None
106	    if masked:
107	        payload = bytes(b ^ mask_key[i % 4] for i, b in enumerate(payload))
108	    return opcode, payload
109	
110	
111	def _recv_exact(sock: Any, n: int) -> bytes | None:
112	    """Read exactly *n* bytes from *sock*, returning None on short read / error."""
113	    buf = bytearray()
114	    while len(buf) < n:
115	        try:
116	            chunk = sock.recv(n - len(buf))
117	        except OSError:
118	            return None
119	        if not chunk:
120	            return None
121	        buf.extend(chunk)
122	    return bytes(buf)
123	
124	
125	def ws_send_text(sock: Any, text: str) -> None:
126	    """Send an unmasked text frame over *sock*."""
127	    payload = text.encode()
128	    header = bytearray()
129	    header.append(0x81)  # FIN + text opcode
130	    length = len(payload)
131	    if length < 126:
132	        header.append(length)
133	    elif length < 65536:
134	        header.append(126)
135	        header.extend(struct.pack("!H", length))
136	    else:
137	        header.append(127)
138	        header.extend(struct.pack("!Q", length))
139	    sock.sendall(bytes(header) + payload)
140	
141	
142	def ws_send_close(sock: Any, code: int = 1000) -> None:
143	    """Send a close frame with the given status code."""
144	    payload = struct.pack("!H", code)
145	    header = bytearray([0x88, len(payload)])
146	    sock.sendall(bytes(header) + payload)

5136	def should_fallback_to_frontend_index(url_path: str) -> bool:
5137	    """Return whether a request path should load the SPA shell."""
5138	    clean = url_path.split("?")[0].split("#")[0] or "/"
5139	    if clean in {"/", "/index.html"}:
5140	        return True
5141	    if clean == "/assets" or clean.startswith("/assets/"):
5142	        return False
5143	    return PurePosixPath(clean).suffix == ""

5146	class BayesHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
5147	    """HTTP handler that exposes the Bayes Market API over JSON."""
5148	
5149	    def log_message(self, fmt: str, *args: object) -> None:
5150	        """Suppress the default BaseHTTPRequestHandler access log output."""
5151	        return
5152	
5153	    def send_json(
5154	        self,
5155	        data: dict[str, Any],
5156	        status: int = 200,
5157	        extra_headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,
5158	    ) -> None:
5159	        """Write a JSON response body with the standard headers."""
5160	        body = json.dumps(data).encode("utf-8")
5161	        self.send_response(status)
5162	        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
5163	        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(body)))
5164	        self.send_header("Cache-Control", "no-store")
5165	        if extra_headers:
5166	            for key, value in extra_headers.items():
5167	                self.send_header(key, value)
5168	        self.end_headers()
5169	        self.wfile.write(body)
5170	
5171	    def _enforce_write_controls(self, method: str) -> str:
5172	        write_request = resolve_write_request_agent(method, self.path, self.headers)
5173	        if write_request is None:
5174	            return ""
5175	
5176	        enforce_rate_limit(write_request.agent_id)
5177	        return write_request.agent_id
5178	
5179	    def _read_json_body(self) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
5180	        try:
5181	            content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", "0"))
5182	        except ValueError as exc:
5183	            raise ApiError(400, "invalid_content_length", "Invalid Content-Length") from exc
5184	
5185	        if content_length <= 0:
5186	            return None
5187	
5188	        raw_body = self.rfile.read(content_length).decode("utf-8")
5189	        try:
5190	            body = json.loads(raw_body)
5191	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
5192	            raise ApiError(400, "invalid_json", "Invalid JSON") from exc
5193	
5194	        if not isinstance(body, dict):
5195	            raise ApiError(400, "invalid_body", "payload must be an object")
5196	        return body
5197	
5198	    def handle_api(self, method: str) -> None:
5199	        """Execute one API request and translate ApiError into JSON responses."""
5200	        extra_headers: dict[str, str] | None = None
5201	        try:
5202	            body = self._read_json_body() if method in {"POST", "PUT", "PATCH"} else None
5203	            agent_id = self._enforce_write_controls(method)
5204	            payload, status = route_request(method, self.path, body, headers=self.headers)
5205	            if status < 400:
5206	                extra_headers = rate_limit_headers(agent_id)
5207	        except ApiError as exc:
5208	            payload, status = error_payload(exc.code, exc.message, **exc.details), exc.status
5209	            if exc.code == "rate_limit_exceeded":
5210	                retry_after = str(exc.details.get("retryAfterSeconds", 1))
5211	                extra_headers = {"Retry-After": retry_after}
5212	                extra_headers.update(rate_limit_headers(str(exc.details.get("agentId", ""))))
5213	        self.send_json(payload, status, extra_headers=extra_headers)
5214	
5215	    def _serve_public_text_asset(self, url_path: str) -> bool:
5216	        """Serve a small set of explicit text assets from the project root."""
5217	        path = normalize_path(urlparse(url_path).path)
5218	        if path != HELLO_TEXT_ROUTE or not HELLO_TEXT_PATH.is_file():
5219	            return False
5220	
5221	        content = HELLO_TEXT_PATH.read_bytes()
5222	        self.send_response(200)
5223	        self.send_header("Content-Type", "text/plain; charset=utf-8")
5224	        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(content)))
5225	        self.send_header("Cache-Control", "no-store")
5226	        self.end_headers()
5227	        self.wfile.write(content)
5228	        return True
5229	
5230	    def _serve_static(self, url_path: str) -> bool:
5231	        """Try to serve a static file from frontend/dist/. Return True if served."""
5232	        if self._serve_public_text_asset(url_path):
5233	            return True
5234	
5235	        if not FRONTEND_DIST.is_dir():
5236	            return False
5237	
5238	        requested_path = normalize_frontend_page_path(url_path)
5239	        clean = requested_path
5240	        if clean == "/":
5241	            clean = "/index.html"
5242	
5243	        dist_root = FRONTEND_DIST.resolve()
5244	        candidate = (dist_root / clean.lstrip("/")).resolve()
5245	        try:
5246	            candidate.relative_to(dist_root)
5247	        except ValueError:
5248	            return False
5249	
5250	        if not candidate.is_file():
5251	            if not should_fallback_to_frontend_index(clean):
5252	                return False
5253	            candidate = dist_root / "index.html"
5254	            if not candidate.is_file():
5255	                return False
5256	
5257	        if candidate == dist_root / "index.html":
5258	            content = render_frontend_index_html(
5259	                candidate.read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
5260	                requested_path,
5261	                headers=self.headers,
5262	            )
5263	        else:
5264	            content = candidate.read_bytes()
5265	        ext = candidate.suffix.lower()
5266	        mime = MIME_TYPES.get(ext, "application/octet-stream")
5267	        self.send_response(200)
5268	        self.send_header("Content-Type", mime)
5269	        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(content)))
5270	        if candidate == dist_root / "index.html":
5271	            self.send_header("Cache-Control", "no-store")
5272	        elif clean.startswith("/assets/") or ext in {".js", ".css", ".woff2", ".woff", ".ttf", ".png", ".svg", ".ico"}:
5273	            self.send_header("Cache-Control", "public, max-age=31536000, immutable")
5274	        self.end_headers()
5275	        self.wfile.write(content)
5276	        return True

5278	    def _handle_risk_websocket(self, path: str) -> bool:
5279	        """Attempt WebSocket upgrade for /ws/accounts/{id}/risk. Returns True if handled."""
5280	        ws_match = re.match(r"^/ws/accounts/([^/]+)/risk$", path)
5281	        if ws_match is None:
5282	            return False
5283	        if (self.headers.get("Upgrade") or "").lower() != "websocket":
5284	            return False
5285	        client_key = self.headers.get("Sec-WebSocket-Key")
5286	        if not client_key:
5287	            self.send_error(400, "Missing Sec-WebSocket-Key")
5288	            return True
5289	
5290	        account_id = ws_match.group(1)
5291	
5292	        # Send 101 Switching Protocols
5293	        accept = ws_accept_key(client_key)
5294	        self.send_response(101, "Switching Protocols")
5295	        self.send_header("Upgrade", "websocket")
5296	        self.send_header("Connection", "Upgrade")
5297	        self.send_header("Sec-WebSocket-Accept", accept)
5298	        self.end_headers()
5299	
5300	        sock = self.request
5301	        ws_register_risk(account_id, sock)
5302	        try:
5303	            # Send initial risk+exposure snapshot
5304	            risk = ACCOUNT_RISK.get(account_id)
5305	            exposure = ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE.get(account_id)
5306	            snapshot_msg: dict[str, Any] = {
5307	                "type": "snapshot",
5308	                "accountId": account_id,
5309	                "risk": deepcopy(risk) if risk is not None else None,
5310	                "exposure": deepcopy(exposure) if exposure is not None else None,
5311	                "seq": _next_ws_seq(),
5312	                "emittedAt": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z"),
5313	            }
5314	            ws_send_text(sock, json.dumps(snapshot_msg))
5315	
5316	            # Read loop: handle ping/pong/close, ignore data frames
5317	            while True:
5318	                frame = ws_read_frame(sock)
5319	                if frame is None:
5320	                    break
5321	                opcode, data = frame
5322	                if opcode == 0x8:  # close
5323	                    try:
5324	                        ws_send_close(sock)
5325	                    except OSError:
5326	                        pass
5327	                    break
5328	                if opcode == 0x9:  # ping → pong
5329	                    try:
5330	                        pong = bytearray([0x8A, len(data)]) + data
5331	                        sock.sendall(bytes(pong))
5332	                    except OSError:
5333	                        break
5334	        except OSError:
5335	            pass
5336	        finally:
5337	            ws_unregister_risk(account_id, sock)
5338	        return True
5339

5396	                        pass
5397	                    break
5398	                if opcode == 0x9:  # ping → pong
5399	                    try:
5400	                        pong = bytearray([0x8A, len(data)]) + data
5401	                        sock.sendall(bytes(pong))
5402	                    except OSError:

5410	    def _handle_websocket(self, path: str) -> bool:
5411	        """Attempt WebSocket upgrade for /ws/markets/{id}/prices. Returns True if handled."""
5412	        ws_match = re.match(r"^/ws/markets/([^/]+)/prices$", path)
5413	        if ws_match is None:
5414	            return False
5415	        if (self.headers.get("Upgrade") or "").lower() != "websocket":
5416	            return False
5417	        client_key = self.headers.get("Sec-WebSocket-Key")
5418	        if not client_key:
5419	            self.send_error(400, "Missing Sec-WebSocket-Key")
5420	            return True
5421	
5422	        market_id = ws_match.group(1)
5423	
5424	        # Send 101 Switching Protocols
5425	        accept = ws_accept_key(client_key)
5426	        self.send_response(101, "Switching Protocols")
5427	        self.send_header("Upgrade", "websocket")
5428	        self.send_header("Connection", "Upgrade")
5429	        self.send_header("Sec-WebSocket-Accept", accept)
5430	        self.end_headers()
5431	
5432	        sock = self.request
5433	        ws_register(market_id, sock)
5434	        try:
5435	            # Send an initial price snapshot immediately upon connection
5436	            market = MARKETS.get(market_id)
5437	            if market is not None:
5438	                msg: dict[str, Any] = {
5439	                    "marketId": market_id,
5440	                    "status": market.get("status", "active"),
5441	                    "marginals": dict(market.get("marginals", {})),
5442	                    "seq": _next_ws_seq(),
5443	                    "emittedAt": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z"),
5444	                    "approxFlag": False,
5445	                }
5446	                resolution = market.get("resolution")
5447	                if resolution is not None:
5448	                    msg["resolution"] = resolution
5449	                res_probs = market.get("resolutionProbabilities")
5450	                if res_probs is not None:
5451	                    msg["resolutionProbabilities"] = dict(res_probs)
5452	                ws_send_text(sock, json.dumps(msg))
5453	
5454	            # Read loop: handle ping/pong/close, ignore data frames
5455	            while True:
5456	                frame = ws_read_frame(sock)
5457	                if frame is None:
5458	                    break
5459	                opcode, data = frame
5460	                if opcode == 0x8:  # close
5461	                    try:
5462	                        ws_send_close(sock)
5463	                    except OSError:
5464	                        pass
5465	                    break
5466	                if opcode == 0x9:  # ping → pong
5467	                    try:
5468	                        pong = bytearray([0x8A, len(data)]) + data
5469	                        sock.sendall(bytes(pong))
5470	                    except OSError:
5471	                        break
5472	        except OSError:
5473	            pass
5474	        finally:
5475	            ws_unregister(market_id, sock)
5476	        return True

5478	    def do_GET(self) -> None:  # noqa: N802
5479	        """Serve API routes first, fall back to static frontend files."""
5480	        path = normalize_path(urlparse(self.path).path)
5481	        if self._handle_events_websocket(path):
5482	            return
5483	        if self._handle_risk_websocket(path):
5484	            return
5485	        if self._handle_websocket(path):
5486	            return
5487	        if path.startswith("/v1/") or path in PUBLIC_HEALTH_ROUTES:
5488	            self.handle_api("GET")
5489	            return
5490	        if path == "/" and "application/json" in (self.headers.get("Accept") or ""):
5491	            self.handle_api("GET")
5492	            return
5493	        if not self._serve_static(self.path):
5494	            self.handle_api("GET")
5495	
5496	    def do_POST(self) -> None:  # noqa: N802
5497	        """Serve an HTTP POST request."""
5498	        self.handle_api("POST")
5499	
5500	    def do_PUT(self) -> None:  # noqa: N802
5501	        """Serve an HTTP PUT request."""
5502	        self.handle_api("PUT")
5503	
5504	    def do_DELETE(self) -> None:  # noqa: N802
5505	        """Serve an HTTP DELETE request."""
5506	        self.handle_api("DELETE")

5509	def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
5510	    """Parse CLI arguments for the standalone backend server."""
5511	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Bayes Market backend server")
5512	    parser.add_argument("--host", default=os.environ.get("BAYES_MARKET_HOST", "127.0.0.1"))
5513	    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=int(os.environ.get("BAYES_MARKET_PORT", "3205")))
5514	    return parser.parse_args()
5515	
5516	
5517	def run_server(host: str = "127.0.0.1", port: int = 3205) -> None:
5518	    """Start the HTTP server and block forever."""
5519	    ThreadingHTTPServer((host, port), BayesHandler).serve_forever()
5520	
5521	
5522	def main() -> int:
5523	    """Run the backend from the command line and return an exit code."""
5524	    args = parse_args()
5525	    run_server(host=args.host, port=args.port)
5526	    return 0
5527	
5528	
5529	if __name__ == "__main__":
5530	    raise SystemExit(main())

4642	def handle_probability_edit(market_id: str, payload: dict[str, Any] | None) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], int]:
4643	    """Handle the full ProbabilityEdit request lifecycle for one market."""
4644	    body = payload if payload is not None else {}
4645	    if not isinstance(body, dict):
4646	        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_body", "payload must be an object")
4647	
4648	    account_id = body.get("accountId")
4649	    if not isinstance(account_id, str) or not account_id.strip():
4650	        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_probability_edit", "accountId is required", {"field": "accountId"})
4651	
4652	    idempotency_key = body.get("idempotencyKey")
4653	    if idempotency_key is not None:
4654	        if not isinstance(idempotency_key, str) or not idempotency_key.strip():
4655	            raise ApiError(
4656	                400,
4657	                "invalid_probability_edit",
4658	                "idempotencyKey must be a non-empty string when provided",
4659	                {"field": "idempotencyKey"},
4660	            )
4661	        idempotency_key = idempotency_key.strip()
4662	
4663	    account_id = account_id.strip()
4664	    scope_key = idempotency_scope_key(market_id, account_id, idempotency_key) if idempotency_key is not None else None
4665	    with get_market_write_lock(market_id):
4666	        normalized_payload = normalize_probability_edit_payload(market_id, body)
4667	        if scope_key is not None:
4668	            existing_command_id = IDEMPOTENCY_KEYS.get(scope_key)
4669	            if existing_command_id is not None:
4670	                existing_command = COMMANDS[existing_command_id]
4671	                if existing_command["payload"] != normalized_payload:
4672	                    return build_idempotency_conflict_response(
4673	                        existing_command_id,
4674	                        idempotency_key,
4675	                        market_id,
4676	                        account_id,
4677	                        "ProbabilityEdit",
4678	                    )
4679	                return replay_terminal_outcome(existing_command_id)
4680	
4681	        submitted_at = utc_timestamp()
4682	        command = materialize_probability_edit_command(
4683	            market_id=market_id,
4684	            normalized_payload=normalized_payload,
4685	            account_id=account_id,
4686	            command_id=generate_command_id(),
4687	            submitted_at=submitted_at,
4688	            idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
4689	        )
4690	        market = MARKETS[market_id]
4691	        if market["status"] != "active":
4692	            return build_terminal_rejection_response(
4693	                command,
4694	                code="market_not_active",
4695	                message="ProbabilityEdit is only allowed for active markets",
4696	                details={
4697	                    "marketId": market_id,
4698	                    "status": market["status"],
4699	                    "allowedStatus": "active",
4700	                    "commandId": command["commandId"],
4701	                },
4702	                retry_hint="submit against an active market",
4703	                status=409,
4704	                scope_key=scope_key,
4705	            )
4706	        preview: dict[str, Any] | None = None
4707	        if not normalized_payload["context"]:

4181	def build_terminal_acceptance_response(
4182	    command: dict[str, Any],
4183	    order: dict[str, Any],
4184	    asset_delta: dict[str, Any],
4185	    scope_key: tuple[str, str, str] | None = None,
4186	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], int]:
4187	    """Emit and persist a terminal acceptance response for a probability edit."""
4188	    target = order["payload"]["target"]
4189	    delta = {
4190	        "variableId": order["payload"]["variableId"],
4191	        "outcomeId": target["outcomeId"],
4192	        "before": order["previousMarginals"][target["outcomeId"]],
4193	        "after": order["newMarginals"][target["outcomeId"]],
4194	    }
4195	    if order["payload"]["context"]:
4196	        delta["context"] = deepcopy(order["payload"]["context"])
4197	
4198	    return build_terminal_response(
4199	        command,
4200	        event_type="CommandAccepted",
4201	        event_payload={
4202	            "effects": {
4203	                "marginalDelta": [delta],
4204	                "assetDelta": [deepcopy(asset_delta)],
4205	            },
4206	            "pricing": {
4207	                "cost": order["impactScore"],
4208	                "fee": 0.0,
4209	            },
4210	            "replayStateHash": market_replay_state_hash(str(command["marketId"])),
4211	        },
4212	        status=201,
4213	        response_fields={
4214	            "order": deepcopy(order),
4215	        },
4216	        scope_key=scope_key,
4217	    )

4220	def settle_market_account_risk(market_id: str, timestamp: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
4221	    """Release current market exposure from account risk and LMSR read models."""
4222	    asset_deltas: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
4223	
4224	    for account_id in sorted(ACCOUNT_RISK):
4225	        account = ACCOUNT_RISK[account_id]
4226	        markets = account.get("markets")
4227	        if not isinstance(markets, dict):
4228	            continue
4229	
4230	        removed_market = markets.pop(market_id, None)
4231	        lmsr_state = ensure_account_lmsr_state(account)
4232	        slices = lmsr_state["slices"]
4233	        slice_keys_to_remove = [
4234	            slice_key
4235	            for slice_key, slice_state in list(slices.items())
4236	            if isinstance(slice_state, dict) and str(slice_state.get("marketId")) == market_id
4237	        ]
4238	        for slice_key in slice_keys_to_remove:
4239	            del slices[slice_key]
4240	
4241	        if removed_market is None and not slice_keys_to_remove:
4242	            continue
4243	
4244	        before_min_asset = round_risk_value(float(account["minAsset"]))
4245	        risk_limit = round_risk_value(float(account["riskLimit"]))
4246	        remaining_consumed = round_risk_value(
4247	            sum(float(market_state["capacityConsumed"]) for market_state in markets.values())
4248	        )
4249	        after_min_asset = round_risk_value(risk_limit - remaining_consumed)
4250	        account["minAsset"] = after_min_asset
4251	        account["updatedAt"] = timestamp
4252	
4253	        if removed_market is not None:
4254	            asset_deltas.append(
4255	                {
4256	                    "accountId": account_id,
4257	                    "marketId": market_id,
4258	                    "beforeMinAsset": before_min_asset,
4259	                    "afterMinAsset": after_min_asset,
4260	                }
4261	            )
4262	
4263	    return asset_deltas
4264

655	def resolve_write_route_category(method: str, raw_path: str) -> str | None:
656	    """Resolve the frozen T582 write-route category for a request, if any."""
657	    if method != "POST":
658	        return None
659	
660	    parsed = urlparse(raw_path)
661	    path = normalize_path(parsed.path)
662	    if path == "/v1/markets":
663	        return MARKET_ADMIN_WRITE_CATEGORY
664	
665	    parts = [part for part in path.split("/") if part]
666	    if len(parts) < 4 or parts[:2] != ["v1", "markets"]:
667	        return None
668	
669	    if len(parts) == 4 and parts[3] == "resolve":
670	        return MARKET_ADMIN_WRITE_CATEGORY
671	    if len(parts) == 4 and parts[3] == "comments":
672	        return TRADE_WRITE_CATEGORY
673	    if len(parts) == 5 and parts[3:] in (["orders", "probability-edit"], ["orders", "event-trade"]):
674	        return TRADE_WRITE_CATEGORY
675	    return None

689	def _invalid_agent_id_reason(raw_agent_id: str, agent_id: str) -> str | None:
690	    """Return the stable validation reason for one protected-write agent id value."""
691	    if "," in raw_agent_id:
692	        return "multiple_values"

698	def _normalize_write_request_agent_id(
699	    raw_values: list[str],
700	    *,
701	    category: str,
702	    require_agent_id: bool,
703	) -> str:
704	    """Normalize one protected-write agent id and preserve legacy error contracts."""
705	    if not raw_values:
706	        enforce_agent_id("", category=category)
707	        return ""
708	
709	    if len(raw_values) > 1:
710	        if require_agent_id:
711	            _raise_invalid_agent_id(category, reason="multiple_values")
712	        return ""
713	
714	    raw_agent_id = raw_values[0]
715	    agent_id = raw_agent_id.strip()
716	    if not agent_id:
717	        if require_agent_id:
718	            raise ApiError(
719	                401,
720	                "blank_agent_id",
721	                "Blank Bayes agent id header",
722	                {"header": AGENT_ID_HEADER, "category": category},
723	            )
724	        return ""
725	
726	    invalid_reason = _invalid_agent_id_reason(raw_agent_id, agent_id)
727	    if invalid_reason is not None:
728	        if require_agent_id:
729	            _raise_invalid_agent_id(category, reason=invalid_reason)
730	        return ""
731	
732	    return agent_id

736	    """Resolve and validate the agent identity for the frozen protected write surface."""

740	
741	    policy = WRITE_ROUTE_POLICIES[category]
742	    require_agent_id = write_policy_requires_agent_id(policy)

744	        request_agent_id_values(headers),

747	    )
748	    return WriteRequestAgentContext(category=category, policy=policy, agent_id=agent_id)

751	def enforce_rate_limit(agent_id: str) -> None:
752	    """Apply the configured per-agent sliding-window rate limit."""
753	    if RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN <= 0 or not agent_id:
754	        return
755	
756	    now = time.monotonic()
757	
758	    with _RATE_LIMIT_LOCK:
759	        bucket = _RATE_LIMIT_WINDOWS.get(agent_id)
760	        if bucket is None:
761	            bucket = deque()
762	            _RATE_LIMIT_WINDOWS[agent_id] = bucket
763	
764	        cutoff = now - RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS
765	        while bucket and bucket[0] <= cutoff:
766	            bucket.popleft()
767	
768	        if len(bucket) >= RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN:
769	            retry_after_seconds = max(1, math.ceil(RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS - (now - bucket[0])))
770	            raise ApiError(
771	                429,
772	                "rate_limit_exceeded",
773	                "Rate limit exceeded for Bayes agent id",
774	                {
775	                    "agentId": agent_id,
776	                    "limit": RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN,
777	                    "windowSeconds": RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS,
778	                    "retryAfterSeconds": retry_after_seconds,
779	                    "policyVersion": RATE_LIMIT_POLICY_VERSION,
780	                },
781	            )
782	
783	        bucket.append(now)
784	
785	
786	def rate_limit_headers(agent_id: str) -> dict[str, str]:
787	    """Build response headers describing the current agent rate-limit state."""
788	    if RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN <= 0 or not agent_id:
789	        return {}
790	
791	    now = time.monotonic()
792	    now_epoch = time.time()
793	    cutoff = now - RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS
794	
795	    with _RATE_LIMIT_LOCK:
796	        bucket = _RATE_LIMIT_WINDOWS.get(agent_id)
797	        active_entries = [] if bucket is None else [timestamp for timestamp in bucket if timestamp > cutoff]
798	
799	    used = len(active_entries)
800	    remaining = max(0, RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN - used)
801	    if active_entries:
802	        seconds_until_reset = max(0.0, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS - (now - active_entries[0]))
803	    else:
804	        seconds_until_reset = float(RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS)
805	
806	    return {
807	        "X-RateLimit-Limit": str(RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN),
808	        "X-RateLimit-Remaining": str(remaining),
809	        "X-RateLimit-Reset": str(math.ceil(now_epoch + seconds_until_reset)),
810	        "X-RateLimit-Policy": RATE_LIMIT_POLICY_VERSION,
811	    }

166	def build_reference_joint_distribution(
167	    market_ids: tuple[str, ...] = REFERENCE_NET_MARKET_IDS,
168	) -> dict[tuple[str, ...], float]:
169	    outcome_grid = [tuple(outcome["id"] for outcome in server.MARKETS[market_id]["outcomes"]) for market_id in market_ids]
170	    joint: dict[tuple[str, ...], float] = {}
171	
172	    for state in itertools.product(*outcome_grid):
173	        probability = 1.0
174	        for market_id, outcome_id in zip(market_ids, state):
175	            probability *= float(server.MARKETS[market_id]["marginals"][outcome_id])
176	        joint[state] = probability

185	def state_matches_context(
186	    market_ids: tuple[str, ...],
187	    state: tuple[str, ...],
188	    context_assignments: dict[str, str],
189	) -> bool:
190	    return all(state[market_ids.index(market_id)] == outcome_id for market_id, outcome_id in context_assignments.items())

193	def brute_force_conditional_marginals(
194	    joint: dict[tuple[str, ...], float],
195	    target_market_id: str,
196	    context: list[dict[str, str]],
197	    market_ids: tuple[str, ...] = REFERENCE_NET_MARKET_IDS,
198	) -> dict[str, float]:
199	    context_assignments = context_assignments_by_market_id(context)
200	    target_index = market_ids.index(target_market_id)
201	    totals = {
202	        outcome["id"]: 0.0
203	        for outcome in server.MARKETS[target_market_id]["outcomes"]
204	    }
205	    context_mass = 0.0
206	
207	    for state, probability in joint.items():
208	        if not state_matches_context(market_ids, state, context_assignments):
209	            continue
210	        context_mass += probability
211	        totals[state[target_index]] += probability
212	
213	    return {outcome_id: probability / context_mass for outcome_id, probability in totals.items()}
214	
215	
216	def brute_force_apply_probability_edit(
217	    joint: dict[tuple[str, ...], float],
218	    target_market_id: str,
219	    outcome_id: str,
220	    probability: float,
221	    context: list[dict[str, str]],
222	    market_ids: tuple[str, ...] = REFERENCE_NET_MARKET_IDS,
223	) -> dict[tuple[str, ...], float]:
224	    context_assignments = context_assignments_by_market_id(context)
225	    target_index = market_ids.index(target_market_id)
226	    before_marginals = brute_force_conditional_marginals(joint, target_market_id, context, market_ids=market_ids)
227	    current_probability = before_marginals[outcome_id]
228	    target_scale = probability / current_probability
229	    non_target_scale = (1.0 - probability) / (1.0 - current_probability)
230	    updated_joint: dict[tuple[str, ...], float] = {}
231	
232	    for state, state_probability in joint.items():
233	        if state_matches_context(market_ids, state, context_assignments):
234	            scale = target_scale if state[target_index] == outcome_id else non_target_scale
235	            updated_joint[state] = state_probability * scale
236	        else:
237	            updated_joint[state] = state_probability
238	
239	    return updated_joint
240

242	def build_random_context(target_market_id: str, rng: random.Random) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
243	    candidate_market_ids = [market_id for market_id in REFERENCE_NET_MARKET_IDS if market_id != target_market_id]
244	    rng.shuffle(candidate_market_ids)
245	    selected_market_ids = candidate_market_ids[: rng.randint(0, len(candidate_market_ids))]
246	    return [
247	        {
248	            "variableId": server.MARKETS[market_id]["variableId"],
249	            "outcomeId": rng.choice(
250	                [
251	                    outcome["id"]
252	                    for outcome in server.MARKETS[market_id]["outcomes"]
253	                    if float(server.MARKETS[market_id]["marginals"][outcome["id"]]) > 0.0
254	                ]
255	            ),
256	        }
257	        for market_id in sorted(selected_market_ids, key=lambda market_id: server.MARKETS[market_id]["variableId"])
258	    ]

269	    for outcome_id, expected_probability in expected.items():
270	        test_case.assertAlmostEqual(actual[outcome_id], expected_probability, delta=delta)

## 7. ERROR RESPONSE FORMAT

**Standard Error Envelope** (lines 1031-1040):

```json
    "code": "error_code_string",

      "field": "value",
      "...": "..."
    }
  },
  "meta": {
    "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
    "version": "version"
  }
}
```

**Common Error Codes:**
- `400 invalid_body` - Malformed JSON
- `400 invalid_json` - JSON parse error
- `400 invalid_probability_edit` - Missing accountId or bad target.probability
- `400 invalid_event_trade` - Formula or trade validation failure
- `400 invalid_comment` - Bad comment body
- `400 invalid_query` - Bad query parameters
- `400 invalid_event_formula` - Formula structure invalid
- `400 position_limit_exceeded` - Position size exceeds market limit
- `401 missing_agent_id` - Required header missing
- `401 invalid_agent_id` - Header format invalid
- `404 not_found` - Resource not found
- `404 market_not_found` - Market doesn't exist
- `409 min_asset_violation` - Would drive asset negative (solvency check)
- `409 market_not_active` - Market status is not "active"
- `409 market_already_exists` - Title/variableId collision
- `429 rate_limit_exceeded` - Rate limit hit
- `501 event_trade_inference_unavailable` - Complex formula not yet supported

**Common Error Codes:**
- `400 invalid_body` - Malformed JSON
- `400 invalid_json` - JSON parse error
- `400 invalid_probability_edit` - Missing accountId or bad target.probability
- `400 invalid_event_trade` - Formula or trade validation failure
- `400 invalid_comment` - Bad comment body
- `400 invalid_query` - Bad query parameters
- `400 invalid_event_formula` - Formula structure invalid
- `400 position_limit_exceeded` - Position size exceeds market limit
- `401 missing_agent_id` - Required header missing
- `401 invalid_agent_id` - Header format invalid
- `404 not_found` - Resource not found
- `404 market_not_found` - Market doesn't exist
- `409 min_asset_violation` - Would drive asset negative (solvency check)
- `409 market_not_active` - Market status is not "active"
- `409 market_already_exists` - Title/variableId collision
- `429 rate_limit_exceeded` - Rate limit hit
- `501 event_trade_inference_unavailable` - Complex formula not yet supported

---

2175	def compute_orders_pnl(market_id: str, account_id: str) -> dict[str, dict[str, float]]:
2176	    """Scan ORDERS and accumulate per-outcome net_size and cost_basis for one account in one market.
2177	
2178	    For EventTrade orders, notional is added (buy) or subtracted (sell) from cost basis,
2179	    and size is added/subtracted from net_size for the target outcome.
2180	
2181	    For ProbabilityEdit orders, impactScore is attributed proportionally across outcomes
2182	    based on absolute marginal changes.
2183	
2184	    Returns a dict keyed by outcome_id with {"netSize": float, "costBasis": float}.
2185	    """
2186	    outcomes: dict[str, dict[str, float]] = {}
2187	
2188	    for order in ORDERS.values():
2189	        if str(order.get("marketId")) != market_id or str(order.get("accountId")) != account_id:
2190	            continue
2191	
2192	        order_type = str(order.get("type", ""))
2193	
2194	        if order_type == "EventTrade":
2195	            outcome_id = str(order["targetOutcomeId"])
2196	            if outcome_id not in outcomes:
2197	                outcomes[outcome_id] = {"netSize": 0.0, "costBasis": 0.0}
2198	
2199	            size = float(order["size"])
2200	            notional = float(order["notional"])
2201	            side = str(order["side"])
2202	
2203	            if side == "buy":
2204	                outcomes[outcome_id]["netSize"] += size
2205	                outcomes[outcome_id]["costBasis"] += notional
2206	            elif side == "sell":
2207	                outcomes[outcome_id]["netSize"] -= size
2208	                outcomes[outcome_id]["costBasis"] -= notional
2209	
2210	        elif order_type == "ProbabilityEdit":
2211	            impact_score = float(order["impactScore"])
2212	            previous = order["previousMarginals"]
2213	            new_marginals = order["newMarginals"]
2214	
2215	            abs_changes: dict[str, float] = {}
2216	            total_abs_change = 0.0
2217	            for oid in previous:
2218	                change = abs(float(new_marginals.get(oid, 0.0)) - float(previous.get(oid, 0.0)))
2219	                abs_changes[oid] = change
2220	                total_abs_change += change
2221	
2222	            if total_abs_change > 0.0:
2223	                for oid, change in abs_changes.items():
2224	                    if change > 0.0:
2225	                        if oid not in outcomes:
2226	                            outcomes[oid] = {"netSize": 0.0, "costBasis": 0.0}
2227	                        outcomes[oid]["costBasis"] += impact_score * (change / total_abs_change)
2228	
2229	    return outcomes

2511	    title = body.get("title")
2512	    description = body.get("description", "")
2513	    outcomes = body.get("outcomes")
2514	    expires_at = body.get("expires_at")
2515	    liquidity = body.get("liquidity", 10000.0)
2516	    max_pos_size = body.get("maxPositionSize")
2517	
2518	    if not title or not isinstance(title, str):
2519	        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "title is required and must be a string")
2520	    if not outcomes or not isinstance(outcomes, list) or len(outcomes) < 2:
2521	        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "outcomes must be a list with at least 2 entries")
2522	    for o in outcomes:
2523	        if not isinstance(o, dict) or "id" not in o or "name" not in o:
2524	            raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "each outcome must have id and name")
2525	    if not expires_at or not isinstance(expires_at, str):
2526	        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "expires_at is required (ISO 8601 string)")
2527	    try:
2528	        parsed_expires = datetime.fromisoformat(expires_at.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
2529	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
2530	        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "expires_at must be a valid ISO 8601 datetime")
2531	    if parsed_expires <= datetime.now(timezone.utc):
2532	        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "expires_at must be in the future")
2533	    if not isinstance(liquidity, (int, float)) or liquidity <= 0:
2534	        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "liquidity must be a positive number")
2535	    for o in outcomes:
2536	        name = o.get("name", "")
2537	        if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
2538	            raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "each outcome name must be a non-empty string")
2539	    if max_pos_size is not None:
2540	        if not isinstance(max_pos_size, (int, float)) or max_pos_size <= 0:
2541	            raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "maxPositionSize must be a positive number")
2542	
2543	    # Check for duplicate outcome IDs
2544	    outcome_ids = [o["id"] for o in outcomes]
2545	    if len(outcome_ids) != len(set(outcome_ids)):
2546	        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "outcome IDs must be unique")
2547	
2548	    # Generate variable ID from title
2549	    variable_id = title.lower().replace(" ", "_")[:40]
2550	    existing_market = next(
2551	        (market for market in MARKETS.values() if str(market.get("variableId")) == variable_id),
2552	        None,
2553	    )
2554	    if existing_market is not None:
2555	        raise ApiError(
2556	            409,
2557	            "market_already_exists",
2558	            "A market with this title already exists",
2559	            {
2560	                "title": title,
2561	                "variableId": variable_id,
2562	                "existingMarketId": str(existing_market["id"]),
2563	            },
2564	        )
2565	
2566	    # Generate market ID
2567	    market_num = len(MARKETS) + 1
2568	    market_id = f"m{market_num}"
2569	    while market_id in MARKETS:
2570	        market_num += 1
2571	        market_id = f"m{market_num}"
2572	
2573	    # Uniform prior
2574	    uniform_p = round(1.0 / len(outcomes), 6)
2575	    marginals = {}
2576	    for i, o in enumerate(outcomes):
2577	        if i < len(outcomes) - 1:
2578	            marginals[o["id"]] = uniform_p
2579	        else:
2580	            marginals[o["id"]] = round(1.0 - uniform_p * (len(outcomes) - 1), 6)
2581	
2582	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
2583	    market: dict[str, Any] = {
2584	        "id": market_id,
2585	        "title": title,
2586	        "description": description,
2587	        "variableId": variable_id,
2588	        "status": "active",
2589	        "outcomes": [{"id": o["id"], "name": o["name"]} for o in outcomes],
2590	        "marginals": marginals,
2591	        "liquidity": float(liquidity),
2592	        "volume": 0.0,
2593	        "created_at": now,
2594	        "expires_at": expires_at,
2595	    }
2596	    if max_pos_size is not None:
2597	        market["maxPositionSize"] = float(max_pos_size)
2598	
2599	    MARKETS[market_id] = market
2600	    ensure_market_engine_state(market_id)
2601	
2602	    genesis_event = emit_market_created_event(market_id, market)
2603	
2604	    return {
2605	        "market": deepcopy(market),
2606	        "event": deepcopy(genesis_event),
2607	        "meta": make_meta(),

2693	    """Parse a single boolean query parameter using the API's lowercase wire format."""

2711

2725	    minimum: int,
2726	    maximum: int | None = None,
2727	) -> int:
2728	    """Parse and validate an integer query parameter with bounds."""
2729	    values = query.get(name, [])
2730	    if len(values) > 1:
2731	        raise ApiError(
2732	            400,
2733	            "invalid_query",

2735	            {"parameter": name, "received": values},
2736	        )
2737	
2738	    if not values:

2740	
2741	    raw_value = values[0]
2742	    try:
2743	        value = int(raw_value)
2744	    except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
2745	        raise ApiError(
2746	            400,
2747	            "invalid_query",
2748	            f"{name} must be an integer",
2749	            {"parameter": name, "received": raw_value},
2750	        ) from exc
2751	
2752	    if value < minimum:
2753	        raise ApiError(
2754	            400,
2755	            "invalid_query",
2756	            f"{name} must be greater than or equal to {minimum}",
2757	            {"parameter": name, "received": raw_value, "minimum": minimum},
2758	        )
2759	
2760	    if maximum is not None and value > maximum:
2761	        raise ApiError(
2762	            400,
2763	            "invalid_query",

2765	            {"parameter": name, "received": raw_value, "maximum": maximum},

2768	    return value

2775	def get_market_analytics(market_id: str, query: dict[str, list[str]]) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], int]:
2776	    """Return aggregated analytics for a market: volume, trade count, price history, top traders."""
2777	    if market_id not in MARKETS:
2778	        raise ApiError(404, "market_not_found", "Market not found", {"market_id": market_id})
2779	
2780	    interval_values = query.get("interval", ["1h"])
2781	    interval = interval_values[0] if interval_values else "1h"
2782	    if interval not in _VALID_ANALYTICS_INTERVALS:
2783	        raise ApiError(
2784	            400,
2785	            "invalid_interval",
2786	            f"interval must be one of: {', '.join(sorted(_VALID_ANALYTICS_INTERVALS))}",
2787	            {"parameter": "interval", "received": interval, "allowed": sorted(_VALID_ANALYTICS_INTERVALS)},
2788	        )
2789	
2790	    market = MARKETS[market_id]
2791	    total_volume = float(market.get("volume", 0.0))
2792	
2793	    market_orders = [
2794	        order for order in ORDERS.values()
2795	        if str(order.get("marketId")) == market_id
2796	    ]
2797	    trade_count = len(market_orders)
2798	
2799	    # Build price_history from events bucketed by interval
2800	    interval_seconds = _INTERVAL_SECONDS[interval]
2801	    with _EVENTS_LOCK:
2802	        market_events = [
2803	            event for event in EVENTS.values()
2804	            if str(event.get("marketId")) == market_id
2805	            and str(event.get("eventType")) not in ("MarketCreated",)
2806	        ]
2807	    market_events.sort(key=lambda e: str(e.get("emittedAt", "")))
2808	
2809	    buckets: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
2810	    for event in market_events:
2811	        timestamp_str = str(event.get("emittedAt", ""))
2812	        if not timestamp_str:
2813	            continue
2814	        try:
2815	            from datetime import datetime as _dt, timezone as _tz
2816	            dt = _dt.fromisoformat(timestamp_str.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
2817	            epoch = dt.timestamp()
2818	        except (ValueError, OSError):
2819	            continue
2820	        bucket_epoch = int(epoch // interval_seconds) * interval_seconds
2821	        bucket_key = _dt.fromtimestamp(bucket_epoch, tz=_tz.utc).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
2822	
2823	        # Extract marginals from event payload's marginalDelta or use order's newMarginals
2824	        marginals = None
2825	        payload = event.get("payload", {})
2826	        effects = payload.get("effects", {})
2827	        marginal_deltas = effects.get("marginalDelta", [])
2828	
2829	        # Find the corresponding order for full marginals snapshot
2830	        command_id = str(event.get("commandId", ""))
2831	        if command_id:
2832	            for order in ORDERS.values():
2833	                if str(order.get("commandId")) == command_id and "newMarginals" in order:
2834	                    marginals = dict(order["newMarginals"])
2835	                    break
2836	
2837	        if marginals is None:
2838	            # Fallback: use current market marginals if we can't find order
2839	            marginals = dict(market.get("marginals", {}))
2840	
2841	        buckets[bucket_key] = {"timestamp": bucket_key, "marginals": marginals}
2842	
2843	    price_history = sorted(buckets.values(), key=lambda b: b["timestamp"])
2844	
2845	    # Build top_traders from ORDERS grouped by account
2846	    trader_stats: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
2847	    for order in market_orders:
2848	        account_id = str(order.get("accountId", ""))
2849	        if not account_id:
2850	            continue
2851	        if account_id not in trader_stats:
2852	            trader_stats[account_id] = {"account_id": account_id, "volume": 0.0, "trade_count": 0}
2853	        # Use impactScore (cost) for probability edits, notional for event trades
2854	        cost = abs(float(order.get("impactScore", 0.0) or order.get("notional", 0.0)))
2855	        trader_stats[account_id]["volume"] += cost
2856	        trader_stats[account_id]["trade_count"] += 1
2857	
2858	    # Round volumes
2859	    for stats in trader_stats.values():
2860	        stats["volume"] = round(stats["volume"], 6)
2861	
2862	    top_traders = sorted(
2863	        trader_stats.values(),
2864	        key=lambda t: (-t["volume"], t["account_id"]),
2865	    )[:10]
2866	
2867	    return {
2868	        "market_id": market_id,
2869	        "total_volume": total_volume,
2870	        "trade_count": trade_count,
2871	        "price_history": price_history,
2872	        "top_traders": top_traders,
2873	        "interval": interval,
2874	        "meta": make_meta(),
2875	    }, 200

2966	def kl_divergence(previous: dict[str, float], updated: dict[str, float]) -> float:
2967	    """Compute the KL divergence from one marginal distribution to another."""
2968	    return round(
2969	        sum(
2970	            new * math.log(new / old)
2971	            for outcome_id, new in updated.items()
2972	            if new > 0 and (old := previous.get(outcome_id, 0.0)) > 0
2973	        ),
2974	        6,
2975	    )

1476	    """Classify capacity health from a risk limit and current minimum asset."""

1489	    consumed = round_risk_value(limit - min_asset)
1490	    available = round_risk_value(min_asset)

1493	        "limit": round_risk_value(limit),

input.content:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Bayes Market API
  description: |
    Prediction market engine with LMSR-based pricing, event-sourced command
    processing, and real-time marginal probability updates.

    ## Authentication

    Write endpoints require an `X-Bayes-Agent-Id` header when the server is
    running with `BAYES_REQUIRE_AGENT_ID=true` (the production default).
    The header value must match the token pattern `[!#$%&'*+\-.^_\`|~0-9A-Za-z]+`.

    ## Rate Limiting

    When `BAYES_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN` is set, responses include standard rate
    limit headers (`X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`,
    `X-RateLimit-Reset`, `X-RateLimit-Policy`). Exceeding the limit returns
    `429` with a `Retry-After` header.

    ## WebSocket Endpoints (not covered here)

    Real-time streaming is available at `/ws/markets/{id}/prices`,
    `/ws/markets/{id}/events`, and `/ws/accounts/{id}/risk`. These are not
    representable in OpenAPI 3.0 and are excluded from this specification.
  version: 1.0.0
  contact:
    name: Bayes Market Engineering

servers:
  - url: http://localhost:3205
    description: Local development server

tags:
  - name: Health
    description: Service health and discovery endpoints
  - name: Markets
    description: Market CRUD, metadata, analytics, events, and comments
  - name: Orders
    description: Trading endpoints (ProbabilityEdit, EventTrade)
  - name: Accounts
    description: Account risk, exposure, positions, and P&L

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Paths
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
paths:

  # ---- Health ----

  /:
    get:
      tags: [Health]
      summary: Service index
      description: Returns the public HTTP surface listing all available route groups.
      operationId: getServiceIndex
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Service index payload
          content:
